plainEnglish: Markup should be well-formed: complete tags, proper nesting, no duplicate attributes, and unique IDs. Note: in WCAG 2.2 this criterion is considered always satisfied for HTML/XML because browsers handle these cases—but duplicate IDs can still break ARIA references, so keep IDs unique.
whyItMatters: Duplicate IDs can break ARIA relationships (like aria-labelledby pointing at the wrong element), so assistive tech announces the wrong name or skips content.
quickCheck: Validate your HTML and confirm IDs are unique (axe's duplicate-id rules); broken nesting and duplicate attributes are largely handled by browsers now.
commonMistakes: Reusing the same id on multiple elements, breaking label or ARIA associations (axe duplicate-id).
Duplicate ids on active or focusable elements (axe duplicate-id-active).
Rendering a list where every item hard-codes the same id instead of a unique one.
Pointing aria-labelledby or htmlFor at an id that appears more than once.

codeExamples.label: Duplicate ids break label and ARIA refs
